page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. This server features 4 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz, with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance includes 2500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, making it highly suitable for data-intensive operations. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics. The system achieves top-tier memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and memory bandwidth read-only cached speeds reaching 462.24 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong, placing in the top 25% at 14.44 GB/sec. However, the CPU exhibits limitations in specific computational tasks. The Passmark CPU score falls into the bottom 25%, and the CPU encryption test registers in the bottom 10% at 752.76, reflecting the absence of specialized instruction sets like AVX or AMX. Large-block uncached memory write speeds are measured at 43.63 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i8ge.xlarge offers a highly dense local storage allocation relative to its compute footprint, reducing the need for external block storage attachments. This resource profile is optimal for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space or persistent database storage. Recommended workloads include relational and non-relational databases, caching layers such as Redis, static web serving, and general-purpose applications that benefit from ARM64 architecture efficiency and substantial local NVMe capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring an ARM64-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 2500 GB local NVMe SSD, managed under the Nitro hypervisor with a 4 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks highlight a top-tier memory subsystem, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10%, though CPU-bound encryption and extended instruction tests fall into the lower percentiles. This hardware profile delivers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy applications by bundling high-capacity local NVMe storage with energy-efficient ARM cores. The instance is highly suited for database hosting, caching engines like Redis, and general-purpose workloads requiring significant local storage and memory density.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 2500 GB local NVMe SSD for high-density database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance with 4 dedicated cores, 32 GB RAM, and 2500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance combining dedicated Graviton cores with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and exceptional memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
Bundles 2500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Features 32.0 GB of RAM, delivering a top-tier memory performance profile
Managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for databases, Redis caching, and storage-intensive ARM64 workloads
